# Copilot Instructions

This project uses modular documentation for implementation context. Do not rely on assumptions when generating code changes. Read these files first and follow them in the listed order.

## Source of Truth Modules
1. Requirements: [docs/copilot/01-requirements-specification.md](docs/copilot/01-requirements-specification.md)
2. Architecture: [docs/copilot/02-architecture.md](docs/copilot/02-architecture.md)
3. Entity Design: [docs/copilot/03-entity-design.md](docs/copilot/03-entity-design.md)
4. API Contracts: [docs/copilot/04-api-contracts.md](docs/copilot/04-api-contracts.md)
5. Coding Rules: [docs/copilot/05-coding-rules.md](docs/copilot/05-coding-rules.md)
6. Operational Configuration: [docs/copilot/06-operational-config.md](docs/copilot/06-operational-config.md)

## Working Agreement for AI-Assisted Changes
- Preserve the existing layered architecture: controller -> service -> repository.
- Keep API responses wrapped in `ApiResponse<T>`.
- Keep validation and status code semantics consistent with documented contracts.
- Keep entity fields and enum persistence strategy consistent with entity design docs.
- Update corresponding docs module whenever behavior or contract changes.

## Doc Maintenance Rules
- If endpoint behavior changes, update [docs/copilot/04-api-contracts.md](docs/copilot/04-api-contracts.md).
- If model/state changes, update [docs/copilot/03-entity-design.md](docs/copilot/03-entity-design.md).
- If architecture/conventions change, update [docs/copilot/02-architecture.md](docs/copilot/02-architecture.md) and [docs/copilot/05-coding-rules.md](docs/copilot/05-coding-rules.md).
- Keep this file as an index and governance layer, not a duplicate of module content.

## Scope Notes
- The modules were generated by scanning current project sources under `src/main/java`, `src/main/resources`, and root build configuration.
- Generated files in `target/` are excluded from normative design decisions.
